title: Not what it used to be at Cradlepoint

Long hours for less than industry average pay 4. Promotions are not based on skill or merit, rather who will kiss the ring and stroke egos 5. Rapidly declining business case for NextG. title: A Global Leader in Innovation and Employee Growth
body: A Global Leader in Innovation and Employee Growth
pros: Work-Life Balance: Ericsson believes in work-life balance with flexible working hours and options for remote working. Diversity and Inclusion: Ericsson has a very inclusive culture. Collaboration in diverse teams around the world is highly encouraged.
cons: Fast-Paced Environment: This rapidly changing technological world requires fast and flexible adaptation, which could be overwhelming to most. Complex Processes: Being an international company, getting through bureaucracy and a long process might be tough.

title: Ericsson goes in cycles
pros: Good company to work for when they are making money
cons: The company goes in a seven- to ten-year cycle, depending on what version of cell phone connection is being used worldwide. They do well when the next version is being deployed, Then they go through some lean years when that deployment has been saturated and they are in the development of the next thing

title: Good Benefits

title: Late-stage company based on North American leadership perspective
body: Late-stage company based on North American leadership perspective
pros: Lots of the best minds come to work at Ericsson and there are some truly wonderful people that cycle through. Those that stay are often the ones who find the right words that North American senior leadership likes to here. They like to hear they are great leaders, everything is great, and everything will be great.
cons: Lots of creative, tech-minded, world changers joined the North American company by believing the marketing material. Then after a while they slowly realize it is the same as other companies: Sustainability is a green-washing term, others will take credit for your creativity and accomplishments, and those with constructive criticism will be managed out of the organization.

title: It's never enough
pros: Good experience and pretty good pay
cons: You're given more work and higher expectations at an alarmingly fast rate. You're not paid more to match it and no matter how much extra you take on, its never enough. If you're motivated its easy to get taken advantage of. You can feel the stress of those around you, including your LT.

title: Title: From Thriving to Corporate Machine - My Journey at Ericsson
body: Title: From Thriving to Corporate Machine - My Journey at Ericsson
pros: Team and Culture (Initially): My immediate team was exceptional, fostering a collaborative and supportive environment. Professional Growth: The role offered substantial opportunities for personal and career development, especially in the early days post-acquisition. Product and Innovation: Working with Cradlepoint's technology was a joy, and the product line was innovative, which made selling and supporting it rewarding.
cons: Cultural Shift: The cultural integration with Ericsson led to a loss of the unique, community-driven atmosphere that characterized Cradlepoint. Bureaucracy: As part of a larger organization, decisions slowed, and the bureaucracy increased, which sometimes hindered agility and local responsiveness. Disconnection from Local Impact: The focus shifted from being a significant player in the local economy to a more generalized corporate entity, reducing the sense of community involvement.

title: Great benefits, great skill development
pros: The company invests a lot into their employees. For field staff we received premium gear, state of the art new hire training (both for experienced and new employees), and a great benefits package. Also, a guaranteed 40 hour work week for hourly and performance bonuses.
cons: Hourly pay was a little low but considering everything else offered it was not a bad deal. Also, the company closed field services very abruptly and chaotically, effectively laying off something around 11,000 employees in one go in North America. Not a lot of heads up was given on this, so a lot of people struggled after the layoffs. People effected were not allowed to receive recommendations from Ericsson directly. I saw 10+ year career guys struggling to find a job and starting from scratch. Definitely felt like the company preaches that they put their people first but I didn't see that play out in this instance.

title: Friends in the Business Go Places
body: Friends in the Business Go Places
pros: Pay was good. Customer was great. Learning the products was also great.
cons: Top management does not listen nor do they care about you, your family or the many years that you dedicated your career too. If you are not friends with top Management then you are put at the bottom of the list. No matter how many years in the company you may have, they will work to let you go! I had 25+ years, and they felt it was time. The reason? Just Business. While they keep less experience people and hire NEW people to take your position at way less pay. I saw it the last 5 years I was there, and yes I brought it up time and time again, because I felt someone had to. They let go of many more experience people that were there for ages. Just to replace them with less experience people. The Customer is who suffers the most!

After 5G, OpenRAN will take over and the revenues will continue to decline unless new business is unlocked.

title: Expectations after being acquired
pros: 1) Ericsson provides great medical, retirement and other benefits that small companies may not afford or have access. 2) For the first 2 years, there will be a lot of debate how to integrate your products/new business into the larger company, so changes to the every day activities will be minimal. 3) Access to a large number of global positions (although limited now due to Covid restrictions) 4) There are a lot of opportunities to integrate with other portfolios if you have a clear business differentiator.
cons: If your business was acquired to eliminate competition, expect drastic staff reductions and project be closed after negotiating end of life with previous customers.

title: Disrespectful management, disorganized, toxic environment that does not value employees
body: Disrespectful management, disorganized, toxic environment that does not value employees
pros: Benefits, Flexible schedule to work from home and have modified hours if you are one of the "favorites", Happy Hours almost weekly to ply you with alcohol to hide the depression all the staff have.
cons: HORRIBLE culture of favoritism. This office epitomizes the 90 -10 rule. 10% of the staff or less does any of the work while the rest are allowed to show up late or not at all and do little to no work. This office needs a total makeover from staff and management. TOXIC culture that does not allow growth or upward movement. Instead of addressing problems, they bring in old staff that just continue to repeat the same way of working that no longer works. This company does not allow any work life balance if you are one of the few that are expected to work. Also, when I left the company, they withheld my last paycheck and I had to wait 3 days until the regular pay period to be paid. I was also not allowed to have an exit interview with HR.

title: Good bene's but always downsizing, you feel like a number
body: Good bene's but always downsizing, you feel like a number
pros: Still decent insurance but not as good as it used to be Salary Good products Great Severance packages
cons: Benefits are constantly being dropped like stock purchase, fully paid insurance You feel like a number, it doesn't matter how good you are you could be laid off or worse: forced to move, forced to outsource position Outsourcing like crazy, moving entire products to the cheapest labor source to save money and then wondering why the products die Terrible decisions that never make sense It seems Ericsson now wants to be out of the software biz as they are selling off products, off shoring, heavy use of contractors no opportunity to move up, training is very hard to get/find Playing games during downsizing, forced resignations if you don't: move, if you don't stay to train your offshore replacements, take a position with a contractor, setting long or extended time windows final layoff date (hoping you will leave sooner without severance) watching your friends and coworkers constantly being let go dealing with new people who don't have the right skills or experience

title: A great company to work for with awesome benefits
body: A great company to work for with awesome benefits
pros: Great company to work for - at least for me given the teams I landed in. The benefits are great and the overall culture is awesome (Sweden folks rock!). Very generous PTO policy and competitive salaries.
cons: As with every large company, there are layers upon layers of bureaucracy before you actually get anything done. Ericsson is Project driven, and quick to terminate projects along with staff if things are not going well. I also found that PMs often over-estimate against product team's advice and then later under-deliver by re-adjusting commitments...

title: Great company to work for (joined as contractor, became full time, then contractor)
body: Great company to work for (joined as contractor, became full time, then contractor)
pros: They invest in their people with training. Get to travel and work with people in other countries & cultures. Focused on sustainable environmental issues, open & diverse workplace.
cons: Track record of M&A failures (Marconi, Redback). Use of clunky proprietary tools when OTS alternatives are available, e.g., bug tracking, video conferencing, document repository, collaboration, IT service, etc. Pattern of divesting right after a large investment. For example investing massively in Redback, only to shut it down, keeping a small piece of it in Budapest. For example Boulder and Warrendale.

They move very fast, which can be tough for some, but those who are willing to move quickly can grow and build their skills in an accellerated and meaningful way.

title: Ericsson and its Video business
pros: Not many right now. They are good with their medical benefits and their 401 K employer contribution
cons: THe business itself is not going anywhere. As a two decade veteran of the High tech Industry with over 12 years in the video business, E/// does not have the DNA to be a video business. It is a set of telco geeks who are trying their hand at the video busienss in a completely incoherent fashion. E/// went on a shopping spree to buy their way into the video marketplace in 2014-1016 but they never integrated the pieces into one coherent product offering. The Video hierarchy is headed at the top 4 layers with telco people with precious little understanding of the video business and neither the contacts in the video business. So in my view the long term success of the Video business is very unlikely.

pros: When you are there they have decent benefits. (Or used to) Decent work life balance - at least in the groups I was in. Being South Asian is a big plus to working there. The new CEO seems to be turning things around, but E will never be the 800 lb gorilla again.
cons: From the time I started in 2012 - it was a continual stream of downsizing in North America. Loss loss loss. Ericsson lost it's market leadership due to hubris and bad decisions. The morale was extremely low when I left. Stay a year or two then start looking for another job before they cut you. Don't get complacent.

title: Sinking fast and Swedes get all the lifeboats
body: Sinking fast and Swedes get all the lifeboats
pros: Innovative company; never stands still. I always was presented with new challenges. Friendly, but demanding atmosphere. Pay and benefits are good, and they honor their commitments. Trips to Sweden are fun.
cons: Led by Swedish culture. Nice people, but things move slowly. Swedish employees take the whole summer off, while US employees keep working hard. Then when they need to downsize, Sweden is retained (they can't lay them off), and all the layoffs happen in the US. Ericsson does not seem to appreciate the hard-working Americans as much as if you are born in Sweden.

cons: Management is pretty clueless and they don't have the guts or power to fix the situation we are in right now. Despite having a lot of work right now, we can't hire additional staff. In fact, quite the opposite is happening. We are working long hours and losing people (because maybe the grass is greener on the other side of the fence, or because management refuses to keep good people). Fear of layoffs is pretty high.

title: A once great company off in a different direction
body: A once great company off in a different direction

cons: Questionable choices in acquisitions are haunting the company and management is still trying to recover. Necessary layoffs went way to far (by 50%). Well placed Managers of poor performing or dismantled groups reassigned and promoted to business areas they didn't understand or manage well. Managers are ineffective and fearful of current group head. Revolving door management, good ones leave or fired, bad one stay. Products they believe are the future are dragging the company down while the core money makers are left to languish without proper support. Over-complicated business and customer management systems.

title: By far the best company I have ever worked for.
body: By far the best company I have ever worked for.
pros: Ericsson has some of the best benefits out there. A company paid medical (for employee, and family) plan. Vision, Dental, 401k (100% match). More time off that I knew what to do with. I had 160 hours of just vacation, then there were floating holidays, sick time, family sick time. and personal days. Direct management was fantastic and easy to work with.
cons: Ericsson can get a little arrogant when it comes to working with its customers (Mainly Sprint). There was defiantly a lack of work (that's why I was laid off).
role: Former employee - RF Field Tech I

title: IT Network Engineer
pros: Benefits, remote work, organized, well connected
cons: Constant Lay offs, Slow growth, less promotional oppertunities

title: Head of Real Estate and Facilities
body: Head of Real Estate and Facilities
pros: Benefits are really good. But, will likely change with the drastic headcount reductions. The company is in significant financial crisis.
cons: Constant reorganization, poor management and very political. The new CEO warned that there will be drastic HC reductions. Internal rumor is up to 40% of HC globally.

title: Was great but uncertainty is affecting everyone
body: Was great but uncertainty is affecting everyone
pros: Flexible work schedule Good education benefits good 401k matching
cons: Management missteps have driven stock price down leading to massive layoffs. The awesome health insurance package is being really scaled back starting in 2018.

title: Retirement
pros: The Ericsson deferred compensation retirement plan through MullinTMG and 401K matching are fantastic benefits!
cons: Almost all major business decisions need to go through excruciating consensus and or through Swedish management for approval.

title: Ericsson
pros: Ericsson is a diversified telecommunications company with many opportunities to learn and grow your portfolio of skills and experience. They offer great training and benefits and care greatly about the environment. They may become the leading player in the 5G technology space in the years to come.
cons: In a word, layoffs. They are restructuring and have been for some time. It is my honest belief and hope that they will soon return to stability and industry leadership with the last few changes that they have made.

title: Road warrior
pros: Excellent benefits package, good pay
cons: I was in installation so you go where the work is. I spent at one time on average 26 nights out of the month in a hotel somewhere.

title: Financial Analyst IV
pros: Global company with standard ways of working
cons: Constant Industry ups and downs

title: Solutions Architect
pros: Fair salary and good benefits
cons: I don't have any comments

title: Cuture is very bad
pros: The best thing the company has to offer to employees is great health insurance benefits, this is what keeps people working there.
cons: The company has failed to secure new contracts, so the culture is you never know from one day to the next if you will have a job! Management is very uncaring of their employees and will layoff fulltime employees to hire contractors to come in and take their jobs to save money. Ericsson also has a policy of 80/20 which means 80% of the work has to be done offshore and they move to push everything the can to India so this displaces American workers. They require employees to log hours they do not work and if you do not do this you are placed on a list to be laid off.
role: Former employee - Access Panning Analyst III

title: Ericsson was a great place to work while it lasted
body: Ericsson was a great place to work while it lasted
pros: Ericsson has some of the best benefits of any company out there, seriously gold star. Their pay was comparable or better than other companies. My coworkers were smart and diligent; it was a good environment for achieving results and learning new technologies.
cons: They made some very bad moves, especially in the Media division. They bought part of Microsoft to enhance their position in the IPTV space and then let that management team run wild. They overspent on a next generation cloud based IPTV platform, which over promised features and undelivered on results. It was too late to the market and didnât deliver much over the previous products.

title: Ericsson
pros: The pros is that E/// is a global company with EXCELLENT benefits, stellar in fact.
cons: E/// acquired the company I worked for in an attempt to gain footing in the media streaming/on-demand area. However, they always seemed to be 2 steps behind the tech curve and the media division is struggling.

title: Project Manager
pros: Flexible hours and ability to work remotely on occassion. Great pay.
cons: Silo work structure. Tendency of management to discount work force input

title: Business plan continually updated causing lots of internal changed, high focus on decreasing costs and this means staff.
body: Business plan continually updated causing lots of internal changed, high focus on decreasing costs and this means staff.
pros: Good company to work at. Great benefits but expect that to change next year. Good people, good eork life balance.
cons: Atmosphere of when will my job be cut. Three major reorganizations in last year has caused major changes multiple ongoing. Expect potential decrease in health plan next year as part of cost cuts. Poor q1, q2 results, expect this to continue into 2018.

title: Solutions Architect/ RF Engineer
pros: Free health insurance. Compensation is adiquat.
cons: On the road often, not enough time for family.

title: Making it barely
pros: Great people to work with
cons: pay is not up to standard

title: ICT program director
pros: respecting culture, ease of internal moves, diverse portfolio
cons: extremely turbulent, frequent changes, struggling within the market

title: Hardware Engineer
pros: Good work culture and benefits. especially medical coverage
cons: The key people and any decision has to come from Sweden. Its not easy to move to other teams no matter how good you are. It means you would have to stick to your team and position.

title: Big Corporate
pros: Overall, a great company to work for. Depending on your department and management team, they give ample opportunity to grow. Benefits are impossible to beat. Ample opportunity to network in a company this large.
cons: Workload expectations for certain departments unrealistic (finance, operations, analyst roles). Flexibility and change management lacking, which will hurt in the long run as the industry changes. Want to solve problems and actually see changes in policy or business process made? This is not the place for you, one can do the work to define a solution, but actual change is impossible to implement. Transparency from top to bottom lacking.

title: Ericsson is struggling to transform
pros: Great people, lots of talent and desire to make a difference
cons: Lack of courageous leadership and a willingness to address underlying challenges

title: Stay tuned
pros: Ericsson is a challenging place to work.But it does have great work life balance, and outstanding benefits . Ericsson promotes diversity and encourages self improvement,
cons: Large company has a lot of processes in place which can stifle innovation. Organizations complete with each other within which causes opportunities to be missed,
